# How to Rank \#1 in Google in 2026: The 3-Step SEO Playbook

14,399 views14K views

421

0:00


In this video, we’re going to give you a
step-by-step roadmap to ranking #1 in Google

0:04


in 2026, starting with the first lesson — don’t
ever trust anyone who tells you they can guarantee

0:10


ranking #1 in Google. Sorry to disappoint, but
that’s just way beyond our control as marketers.

0:16


But, before you click off, what you can do is
create as many winning conditions as possible on

0:30


your website to give you the highest-possible
chance of ranking highly in Google.

0:35


That’s a less sexy, but
more accurate way to say it.

0:40


My name is Matt and I run a consultancy called
Kenyon Digital where I have helped clients hit the

0:44


#1 spot for their key terms and have come up with
a repeatable process that can help just about any

0:46


business or brand get there. I’ve partnered with
Surfer to give you that playbook in this video.

0:55


So before we get into the nitty-gritty, we have
to take realistic stock of what we’re after. I’m

1:01


going to give you three reality checks here.
First, If you are looking to rank #1 for

1:06


ultra-competitive terms — terms that have been
dominated by enterprise-level businesses for years

1:12


or decades, that’s probably not a feasible task.
These businesses have spent years building their

1:18


authority and millions of dollars on
their content. It’s not impossible,

1:23


but it’s probably not worth the resources,
especially for smaller to medium-sized businesses.

1:30


Secondly, you have to understand that it’s one
thing to rank #1 and it’s another thing to stay

1:36


#1\. Once you crack that top spot for a particular
keyword, especially a competitive one, you’ve got

1:42


a target on your back, and all your competitors
will be trying outdo you in content quality and

1:48


build more links than you. So, just know that
getting the ranking is half the battle, keeping

1:54


it is an ongoing effort that requires regular
content refreshing, authority-building, and more.

2:00


And thirdly, you don’t need to rank #1 for
every keyword relevant to your business,

2:06


in fact it may be a waste of resources
to try to do so. For any given business,

2:11


there are likely 1,000s of potential keywords
across their service offerings and content,

2:16


and expecting or trying to rank all of them #1
is just not realistic (nor is it necessary to

2:23


have a very successful organic search presence).
One of the hardest things about SEO is not the

2:30


execution, it’s the prioritization, and that’s
something I hope to help you with in this video.

2:36


Okay, so our minds are calibrated, our
expectations are set… let’s get ranking.

2:42


Step #1 - Identifying Your Most Valuable Keywords
Now, I know many of you watching this already

2:44


have a website, likely with some ranking keywords,
whether they’re ranking high or low is a different

2:50


story — but I just want you to forget about
that for a second and start with a clean slate.

2:55


Ask yourself — what are the business-driving
keywords that in a perfect world, my prospects

3:02


would type into Google and then find me.
This is going to be different for different

3:07


types of businesses. But the first is almost
always going to be your brand name. The next

3:12


most important are likely going to be
explicit names of products or services

3:16


you sell or keywords describing those things.
Warby Parker, a direct to consumer eyewear brand,

3:23


targets keywords like:
"buy glasses online"

3:25


"affordable prescription glasses"
"home try-on glasses"

3:30


DocuSign (B2B SaaS Company),
will target keywords like:

3:33


“electronic signature software”
“sign documents online”

3:36


or “DocuSign alternatives” (yes,
you want to rank for this, too)

3:41


And for Nicks Plumbing, a local business in
Houston, TX, target keywords might include:

3:46


“plumber near me”
“Houston plumber”

3:48


or names of specific services like:
“drain cleaning Houston”

3:53


“water heater repair Houston”
or “sewer line repair Houston”

3:57


Once you’ve identified your core keywords,
Google each of them and really study the

4:03


results page. The first thing you’ll notice — if
you haven’t noticed already — is that in 2026,

4:08


the search engine results pages (or SERPs for

4:11


short) look very different than they
did even just a few short years ago.

4:16


For example, when I Google “electronic signature
software,” I see a series of sponsored posts,

4:21


a Reddit discussion as the top organic result,
a listicle of the best electronic signature

4:26


software platforms, and some homepages of
popular software companies, like Docusign.

4:32


Depending on how Google is feeling that day, you
may also see YouTube video results, AI Overviews,

4:39


or Accordion-style People Also Ask questions. So
yeah, we’ve come a long way from 10 blue links.

4:46


It’s easy to get overwhelmed here, but
our main goal is to figure out one thing:

4:51


what is the search intent behind this keyword —
or put another way, what do you think people are

4:58


trying to find when they search this? And perhaps
more importantly, what does Google think people

5:04


are trying to find when they search this? I
call this Google’s Asssumed Search Intent,

5:10


and it’s very important that your content meets
that search intent as closely as possible.

5:17


Now, I’m glad I used this particular query
(”electronic signature software”) as an example,

5:22


because it has mixed-intent, meaning
there are multiple possible intents

5:28


that people can have when searching this.
How do we know this? We look at the SERP.

5:33


Someone searching for this term might want to
eavesdrop on some Reddit discussions about the

5:37


best software for e-signatures. Or, they might
want to read a round-up post comparing a list of

5:42


the best platforms. Or, maybe they just want
to be taken straight to a vendor’s homepage.

5:47


Google, despite its sophistication, doesn’t quite
know, so it gives the user a all-you-can-click

5:54


buffet of options and lets them decide.
But this poses a problem, because I just

6:00


said the most important thing is matching your
page’s content to the keyword’s search intent. So

6:06


how do you do that when search intent is mixed?
There are a few different approaches you can

6:11


take. I’ll explain them and then
share which one I like the best.

6:15


The first is simply adopting the majority view.
If 6 out of 10 results are homepages. You stick

6:21


with targeting that keyword on your homepage.
The second is more strategic, and it involves

6:26


asking what the most likely approach would be
to get you featured on the SERP based on your

6:33


brand’s authority and existing search
presence. This is the approach that Get

6:40


Accept has taken with their round-up post.
See, DocuSign and PandaDoc have massive

6:46


brand recognition, which allows them to rank their
homepage for this keyword. GetAccept doesn’t yet,

7:00


so they kind of find a backdoor approach. Rather
than attempting to go band-for-band on homepages,

7:06


they say, “We’ll just publish a round-up
page giving our users a ton of free value,

7:11


even to the point of mentioning our
competitors, and let them decide.”

7:15


And even though they don’t have the site authority
of PandaDoc or DocuSign, they still manage to

7:20


outrank them with their round-up post.
(Just another reason why round-up

7:24


posts are awesome.)
Step #2 - Build the

7:25


Best Possible Page for That Keyword
Okay, so you've identified your target

7:27


keyword and studied the SERP. You know what Google
thinks users want. Step #2 is to build the best

7:35


possible page for that keyword.
Let's break this down into

7:38


the components that actually matter.
Firstly, matching the format to the intent.

7:44


Webpages come in all different shapes and
sizes. A product page looks and feels quite

7:49


different from a long-form informational
blog post, which likewise looks different

7:54


than an eCommerce category page.
Your job is to match that format of the

8:00


top-ranking results for your target keyword, while
differentiating the substance of that content.

8:07


(And yes, if the format is a video,
which is very common in how-to Google

8:12


searches, you need to be making video!)
And real quick, I don’t know how many more

8:16


times we need to keep saying this, but Google does
not prefer or reward a particular word count. The

8:22


question to ask isn't "how long should this be?"
It's "what does someone searching this term need

8:28


to know, and have I covered it completely?"
For every piece of content I create,

8:33


I start by adding my target keyword into
Surfer’s Content Editor. For this piece,

8:38


I’m targeting “how to start a hydroponic garden.”
I love Surfer’s new step-by-step process here in

8:43


the right-hand panel, which takes the guesswork
out of each step of the writing workflow.

8:48


I’ll usually start by looking through the
competitors’ outlines to get my creative

8:52


juices flowing and make sure I’m covering this
topic at least as thoroughly as my competitors.

8:59


You can also create an AI-generated outline,
which you don’t need to use in its entirety,

9:04


but can be great for giving you some ideas
– and it’s all based on real data from the

9:09


top-ranking pages in the SERP.
As part of my research,

9:12


I also click the Questions tab to make sure
I’m answering all of these in the content.

9:17


After I’ve finished a draft in my own words, I’ll
retroactively go back and make sure I’m including

9:23


all the entities I need to optimize for both
traditional search engines and AI assistants.

9:29


By the way, I think we’re long overdue for
a full content workflow from start to finish

9:33


using Surfer. I don’t have enough time to deep
dive into that right now, but leave a comment if

9:38


that’s something you’d like to see soon.
Now, just having a well-optimized,

9:42


thorough piece of content isn’t enough. The second
phase of creating great content that ranks highly

9:49


is demonstrating real expertise and experience.
You may have heard the acronym EEAT thrown around,

9:56


that’s a Google term that stands for Experience,
Expertise, Authoritativeness and Trustworthiness,

10:02


and it’s how Google claims to evaluate content.
Google has gotten very good at identifying whether

10:09


content comes from first-hand experience
or was just assembled from other sources.

10:14


According to some reputable SEO sources,
the extra "E" — Experience — is now the

10:19


most heavily weighted factor.
What signals real experience?

10:24


Original photos. Screenshots. Step-by-step
documentation from actually doing the thing.

10:30


Case studies with specific numbers. Personal
anecdotes about what worked and what didn't.

10:35


It just so happens that the GetAccept round-up
post we referenced earlier does this very well.

10:41


First, they explicitly establish
authority with this sub-header "Yes,

10:45


we really have tested each platform."
Second, they include original screenshots of

10:50


every single platform. These are presumably not
stock images or marketing graphics pulled from

10:51


company websites, but actual UI screenshots from
inside the products. You can see document editors,

10:53


signature flows, dashboards. These are the kind of

10:56


images you can only get by actually
logging in and using the software.

11:01


Third, they structure each review
consistently: a "Best for" badge,

11:04


a description, a screenshot, pros, cons,
and an ideal use case. That consistency

11:10


shows methodology. It shows they ran each
platform through the same evaluation framework.

11:16


And finally, there's a real author with a
byline and photo. Natasha Ellis-Knight. You

11:22


can see when it was published and when it
was last updated. These are small details,

11:28


but they signal accountability.
Someone's name is attached to this.

11:32


In addition to all these experience signals, they
have a nice table of contents, and a tl;dr section

11:37


making the piece highly convenient for skimming.
So — If you're writing about a product, have you

11:43


actually used it? If you're explaining a process,
have you actually done it? If you're reviewing a

11:49


service, have you actually hired them?
If the answer is no, either get that

11:54


experience or find someone who has
it and involve them in the content.

11:59


I mean heck this even be someone you
find in the reviews of the products

12:03


that you interview for their experience. You
don’t need to be an expert in everything,

12:08


you just need to demonstrate expertise and
experience, even if it’s from someone else.

12:14


Okay, so you’ve matched format to intent,
you’ve built a great page that satisfies

12:19


EEAT signals and… you’re good to go! Be sure
to like and subscribe for more tips and have

12:24


fun making all that money! No, of course not,
if SEO were that easy, brand new websites could

12:31


outrank behemoth brands in a matter of days.
No, unfortunately, building an amazing page

12:36


isn’t nearly enough, especially if you’re a newer
site or one that’s typically gotten lower search

12:42


traffic. You have to power up your pages,
and that’s step #3 in ranking #1 in Google.

12:49


Step #3 - Power Up Your Pages
Okay, so how do we power up our

12:52


money pages such that Google and users trust
us with their clicks? The main ways are:

12:58


By building topical authority around
your target keyword through helpful,

13:02


interrelated content pieces all connected by
internal links (I’ll break that down in a second)

13:08


By acquiring healthy, natural backlinks
from other websites to your website

13:14


Let’s keep it real simple — pretend you’re
Google and you are tasked with evaluating

13:20


whether or not two different websites should rank
for the keyword, “best hydroponic garden system.”

13:26


Both websites have a great page on the topic with
expert advice, engaging on-page elements like

13:31


infographics, and plenty of firsthand experience.
But website A only has one page on the overarching

13:39


topic of hydroponic gardening, freshly published
a couple weeks ago. Website B on the other hand,

13:45


has an entire ecosystem of related content
pieces covering hydroponic gardening from every

13:51


conceivable angle. Best hydroponic gardens for
vegetables, for outdoor growing, for apartments,

13:57


for limited space, best gardens on a
budget — you name it, they have it.

14:02


Not only that but since they’ve developed so much
great content, they’ve earned powerful backlinks

14:07


from sites like Better Homes and Gardens
and a bunch of niche gardening websites.

14:12


Which would you choose to rank for “best
hydroponic garden system”? — keep in mind,

14:18


no difference in quality on the page itself,
but the answer is obvious. We’d choose the

14:23


one with proven trust from other websites
and clear topical authority on the subject.

14:29


This is exactly why Surfer exists and
why I personally use it and have used

14:35


even before making videos on this channel.
Surfer's Sites dashboard takes the guesswork

14:40


out of building topical authority. You plug in
your domain, connect it to Google Search Console,

14:45


and Surfer analyzes your existing
content coverage — showing you exactly

14:49


where you stand and where the gaps are.
It clusters related keywords together and

14:51


visualizes your topical coverage,
so you can see at a glance: "Okay,

14:52


I've covered hydroponic gardens for beginners
pretty well, but I'm completely missing

14:52


content around hydroponic lighting, nutrient
solutions, and troubleshooting common problems."

14:52


Those gaps? Those are your next content
priorities. Each piece you publish fills

14:56


in more of that topical map, signals to
Google that you actually know what you're

15:00


talking about, and creates more opportunities
for internal linking back to your money pages.

15:05


And once you've identified what to
write, Surfer's Content Editor helps

15:08


you build each piece with the right semantic
keywords, proper structure, and comprehensive

15:14


factual coverage from both traditional and AI
Search platforms — so every supporting article

15:20


actually moves the needle on your authority.
The result is a content ecosystem where every

15:26


piece supports every other piece, and
your money pages sit at the center of

15:31


a web of trust signals pointing right at them.
Now, let’s talk about the second portion of that

15:38


“powering up” equation: getting backlinks
from other websites to your website.

15:44


Backlinking is a massive topic that deserves
its own video or even series of videos,

15:49


so as much as I’m tempted, I can’t go down the
rabbit hole today. That said, I do have a video

15:54


on my own channel, Matt Kenyon Marketing, of
my favorite backlink acquisition techniques,

15:59


which you can check out via the info card or
link in the description if you’re interested.

16:04


I will say this though — and I will die
on this hill — I believe it’s far more

16:08


important to build your topical authority with
interrelated pieces of content first before

16:15


you go hunting for backlinks, and here’s why.

one with proven trust from other websites
and clear topical authority on the subject.

14:29


This is exactly why Surfer exists and
why I personally use it and have used

14:35


even before making videos on this channel.
Surfer's Sites dashboard takes the guesswork

14:40


out of building topical authority. You plug in
your domain, connect it to Google Search Console,

14:45


and Surfer analyzes your existing
content coverage — showing you exactly

14:49


where you stand and where the gaps are.
It clusters related keywords together and

14:51


visualizes your topical coverage,
so you can see at a glance: "Okay,

14:52


I've covered hydroponic gardens for beginners
pretty well, but I'm completely missing

14:52


content around hydroponic lighting, nutrient
solutions, and troubleshooting common problems."

14:52


Those gaps? Those are your next content
priorities. Each piece you publish fills

14:56


in more of that topical map, signals to
Google that you actually know what you're

15:00


talking about, and creates more opportunities
for internal linking back to your money pages.

15:05


And once you've identified what to
write, Surfer's Content Editor helps

15:08


you build each piece with the right semantic
keywords, proper structure, and comprehensive

15:14


factual coverage from both traditional and AI
Search platforms — so every supporting article

15:20


actually moves the needle on your authority.
The result is a content ecosystem where every

15:26


piece supports every other piece, and
your money pages sit at the center of

15:31


a web of trust signals pointing right at them.
Now, let’s talk about the second portion of that

15:38


“powering up” equation: getting backlinks
from other websites to your website.

15:44


Backlinking is a massive topic that deserves
its own video or even series of videos,

15:49


so as much as I’m tempted, I can’t go down the
rabbit hole today. That said, I do have a video

15:54


on my own channel, Matt Kenyon Marketing, of
my favorite backlink acquisition techniques,

15:59


which you can check out via the info card or
link in the description if you’re interested.

16:04


I will say this though — and I will die
on this hill — I believe it’s far more

16:08


important to build your topical authority with
interrelated pieces of content first before

16:15


you go hunting for backlinks, and here’s why.
Let’s stick with the hydroponics thing — say

16:20


you’re trying to build a hydroponics supply
brand and you start doing some outreach to other

16:25


websites for backlinks. Of course, my preference
would just be to create content that’s so good

16:30


that other sites link to you naturally, but
everyone gets very upset when you say that.

16:35


Anyway — which would be an easier sell to try to
get a backlink from a site owner? Would it be:

16:40


“Hey, can you please link to my
sales page because I want money” OR

16:45


“Hey, I noticed that you linked to a page
on an old site that hasn’t been updated

16:49


in 6 years on the best hydroponic gardening
nutrient solutions. I just wrote a brand new

16:54


post where I tested a bunch and I think it could
really help your audience, plus, transparently,

16:59


I would honestly love a backlink from your site.”
It’s a no-brainer — they’d almost be silly

17:04


not to link to you given the value
you’re providing to their audience.

17:08


So great, you get the backlink to your blog post,
how does that power up your money pages though,

17:13


how does that help make moneyyyyyy my dude.
Two words. Some of the sweetest words in all

17:21


of SEO. “Internal linking.” More specifically
“Internal linking passing link authority to other

17:29


pages on your site.” That’s 11 words and doesn’t
quite roll off the tongue, but here’s the point:

17:36


When you build content clusters around your
money pages, and then internally link everything

17:41


together, all the backlink authority that each of
those supporting pages get (like our hypothetical

17:47


blog post on nutrient solutions), gets passed up
to the money page through those internal links,

17:56


boosting the authority of the
money page in search results.

17:59


Then, you rinse and repeat, applying this process
to each and every one of your revenue-driving

18:05


pages, and the rising tides of organic
growth lifts the entire ship of your website.

18:12


Are you seeing the power
of this? Isn’t SEO insane?!

18:17


(I didn’t think as a little child who wanted to
make Pixar movies that I’d be saying that about

18:22


a digital marketing strategy but here we are.)
Now this is a lot of hard work — I just described

18:28


doing this for one particular money page.
If you have multiple products or services,

18:32


you’ll be rinsing and repeating for each of them.
I’m condensing this process for the sake of this

18:37


video, but creating just one of these
topical clusters around your money page

18:41


and building links to those pages can
take several months of full-time work,

18:46


and even then there’s no guarantee that you’ll
see results for another few more months.

18:50


And also I’m assuming that you
already have good product-market

18:53


fit and a technically sound website.
But SEO rewards the patient. Because

19:00


traffic gained from SEO is high-intent
and high-quality, and you can often

19:05


reap the rewards of it for years to come.
When you study the patterns of the #1 ranking

19:11


websites, no matter what industry, they follow
this playbook. They build killer money pages with

19:17


compelling copy and engaging visuals, they create
whole ecosystems of content around those pages,

19:24


and build authority and trust from the rest of the
internet, sometimes over the course of decades.

19:30


So that’s the move — it’s not easy, but it’s very
doable, even for small to medium-sized businesses.

19:38


And the best part is that you don't have to do
it alone and you don't have to do it manually.

19:43


Software tools like Surfer exist to handle
the heavy lifting — identifying keyword gaps,

19:48


optimizing your content, and tracking your
progress so you can focus on actually building

19:54


the business. If you want to try it out,
there's a link in the description below.

19:58


And if you want to learn more about growing your
AI Visibility, be sure to watch this video next.

20:04


Thanks for watching, and happy ranking

[![](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/simple-icons@v9/icons/openai.svg) ChatGPT](https://chatgpt.com/?q=Summarize+and+analyze+this+article%3A+The+Most+Important+Google+Ranking+Factors+in+2026+https%3A%2F%2Fservicensure.com%2Fblog%2Fgoogle-ranking-factors-in-2026%2F) [![](https://cdn.simpleicons.org/perplexity) Perplexity](https://www.perplexity.ai/search?q=Summarize+and+analyze+this+article%3A+The+Most+Important+Google+Ranking+Factors+in+2026+https%3A%2F%2Fservicensure.com%2Fblog%2Fgoogle-ranking-factors-in-2026%2F) [![](https://cdn.simpleicons.org/x) Grok](https://grok.com/?q=Summarize+and+analyze+this+article%3A+The+Most+Important+Google+Ranking+Factors+in+2026+https%3A%2F%2Fservicensure.com%2Fblog%2Fgoogle-ranking-factors-in-2026%2F) [![](https://cdn.simpleicons.org/google) Google AI](https://www.google.com/search?udm=50&aep=11&q=Summarize+and+analyze+this+article%3A+The+Most+Important+Google+Ranking+Factors+in+2026+https%3A%2F%2Fservicensure.com%2Fblog%2Fgoogle-ranking-factors-in-2026%2F) [![](https://cdn.simpleicons.org/anthropic) Claude](https://claude.ai/new?q=Summarize+and+analyze+this+article%3A+The+Most+Important+Google+Ranking+Factors+in+2026+https%3A%2F%2Fservicensure.com%2Fblog%2Fgoogle-ranking-factors-in-2026%2F)

Google ranking factors in 2026 have changed significantly. Rather than matching exact keywords, the search engine now prioritizes content that shares in-depth details and is relevant to the search intent. Businesses that neglect the new ranking factors are not able to rise in the competitive market. Furthermore, old practices, like keyword stuffing, can also result in getting penalized. To help ensure that you are able to secure top positions for business in online rankings, we will discuss the most important ranking factors of Google, along with their best practices.

## **What Are The Important Google Ranking Factors?**

![important-google-ranking-factors](https://servicensure.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/important-google-ranking-factors-3.webp)

Google aims to provide the best and most relevant results to its users. Due to this, it often filters out outdated or keyword-stuffed content. Google uses multiple ranking factors to determine which content should appear at the top of the results. Below, we have shared some of the most important Google ranking factors.

### 1\. User Experience

User experience is one of the most important SEO ranking factors. Google’s systems are able to assess how a user interacts with an individual website and its pages. It uses this information to understand whether the site has optimal loading speed, a clean interface, and a mobile-friendly layout. If a website has an optimal user experience, then Google will favor it over websites that don’t.

### 2\. Search Intent Alignment

Google no longer prioritizes content that matches the exact phrases from a search query. Instead, it aims to understand the meaning behind those queries and provide results that best answer them. While it is among the top Google ranking algorithms, it goes far beyond a single broad label. This is because it is divided into four different categories, each of which aims to attract audiences from different stages of the marketing funnel. The four types of search intent are:

- **Informational:** It includes users who are looking for answers, tips, or perhaps guides.
- **Navigational:** The user is trying to navigate to a specific website or a page.
- **Commercial:** Users of this intent are planning to make a purchase but are not yet fully ready.
- **Transactional:** This intent indicates that users are ready to make a purchase.

### 3\. E-E-A-T

E-E-A-T (Expertise, Experience, Authoritativeness, and Trustworthiness) continues to be one of the driving ranking factors. While E-E-A-T is not exactly a part of the algorithm, it is one of the most important Google SEO guidelines. Adhering to E-E-A-T guidelines can also help ensure both relevance and engagement. Higher relevance contributes to building authority over time. Google also analyzes E-E-A-T factors to identify whether the website is properly following the guidelines. Websites that do not adhere to these guidelines will find it difficult to rank well, while the ones that do follow E-E-A-T guidelines will thrive inevitably.

**_Also Read: [What is E-E-A-T and Why Is It Important for SEO?](https://servicensure.com/blog/what-is-e-e-a-t-and-why-is-it-important/)_**

### 4\. Engagement Signals

When it comes to improving online rankings, engagement signals play a crucial role. These engagement signals include click-through rates, dwell time, scroll depth, and repeat visits. Google assesses these signals to determine whether the content is actually helpful or not. Based on this analysis, Google ranks the content on the SERP (Search Engine Results Page).

### 5\. Topical Authority

In 2026, Google page ranking algorithms are increasingly favoring websites that demonstrate clear topical authority. In simple terms, topical authority is a SEO jargon for subject matter expertise. Contrary to domain authority, topical authority is the perceived authority that is built over a niche or a broader subject. According to [**Niel Patel’s guide**](https://neilpatel.com/blog/topical-authority/), AI tools like ChatGPT and Perplexity also use this to determine whether the content should be shown to the user or not.

[![book a free digital marketing consultation](https://servicensure.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/book-a-free-digital-marketing-consultation-1.webp)](tel:+91 98185 84937)

## **Best Practices to Improve Your Google Search Ranking**

While there are multiple ways to improve your Google search ranking organically, one thing you need to understand is that ranking does not improve overnight. Moreover, continuous monitoring and optimization are also required to ensure optimal results. Below, we have listed some key practices that you can follow to attain a higher position in search engine rankings.

![improve-your-google-search-ranking](https://servicensure.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/important-google-ranking-factors-1.webp)

### 1\. Craft Unique Content

Avoiding duplicity is one of the primary rules of content writing. When it comes to crafting unique content, however, it means that the content should provide a unique value to the user. The content should provide in-depth information on the topic. Moreover, a catchy title tag will also contribute significantly to attracting potential customers.

Although crafting multiple unique content can be tricky, especially if you are an eCommerce site owner, you can always take the assistance of organic [**_s_** _**earch engine optimization services**_](https://servicensure.com/search-engine-optimization/) to craft high-quality as well as unique content.

### 2\. Improve User Experience

User experience is one of the most essential factors for website optimization for Google. A poor UX will result in causing your audience to quickly leave your website. If enough people bounce from your website back to search engine results, then this signals Google that your results didn’t provide the users with the answers they were looking for. As a result, your site ranking will drop along with organic traffic.

In order to avoid this scenario from coming true, you will need to optimize scenarios, like.

- **Loading Speed:** Reduce the size of images and use browser caching to improve the overall speed of your website.
- **Mobile-Friendly Design:** Make sure that your website is fully functional and easy to use on mobile devices.
- **Clear Navigation:** Create a clear and hierarchical navigation structure so that your users can quickly find information.
- **White Space:** Prevent overcrowding of sections by effectively using whitespace. This will make the design clean, organized, and easy to understand.
- **Accessibility:** Ensure that your content is easy to understand and provides clear information.

### 3\. Optimize Visuals for SEO

Visuals play a significant role in engaging users with the content and informing them about the product, service, or the entire business. However, visuals need to be optimized with clear names and alt text. This is because the crawlers cannot analyze visuals, and they use visual’s name and alt text to determine the content of the visual.

Therefore, no matter how appealing your visuals may be, they are highly unlikely to come in the top results if they are not optimized. Speaking of optimising visuals, you will also need to ensure that the visuals are clear while being small in size. This will ensure that the visuals load quickly and the user does not leave your website instantly.

### 4\. Add Schema Markup

One of the most renowned SEO optimization tips is adding schema markup. Schema markup helps search engines better understand what the content of your website is about. This not only helps to improve visibility but also results in improving overall engagement. However, in order to get these benefits, you will need to add the right schema type for specific content formats.

**The different types of schema include:**

- **Article Schema:** This schema markup type is specifically for blog posts, news articles, or other written content on your website.
- **FAQ Schema:** As the name suggests, this schema markup is for presenting frequently asked questions in an organized as well as search-optimized way.
- **Event Schema:** This is ideal for highlighting event dates, locations, and ticketing details that you shared on your website.
- **LocalBusiness Schema:** This schema markup is specifically for local businesses as it includes address, working hours, and contact information to boost visibility and attract local customers.

Adding the right type of schema markup is one of the ideal ways to optimize your website for search engines. Furthermore, it will also help your content to stand out with rich snippets, promoting both organic visibility and click-through rates (CTR).

_**Also Read:** [**How To Add Schema Markup To Improve SEO Performance?**](https://servicensure.com/blog/how-to-add-schema-markup/)_

### 5\. Build High Quality Backlinks

Backlinks are a core part of almost every SEO strategy. While backlinks do not directly impact content, they act as a “vote of trust” by earning links from other credible and trustworthy websites within the same niche. One important factor about building backlinks to understand is that the practice is a marathon, not a sprint. Therefore, high-quality backlinks provide much more significant value than those low in quality. Due to this, make sure that your backlink profile only contains optimal quality backlinks. Low-quality backlinks might not even provide any reliable results, not to mention that they often run along with the risk of getting penalized.

## **Leverage Google Ranking Factors to Hold Top Results**

In 2026, Google ranking factors have changed significantly and no longer prioritize results by matching exact phrases. Instead, the ranking is determined by relevance, user satisfaction, authority, and engagement. Google uses multiple metrics and guidelines to rank content. By thoroughly understanding these factors and implementing their relevant practices, you can ensure that your business rises effectively in the online market. However, one crucial thing to understand is that organic ranking may drop overnight; it will not rise in the same duration. Therefore, regular monitoring and optimization become important.

## **FAQs**

**What are the main pillars of SEO?**

There are four main pillars of SEO: On-Page SEO, Off-Page SEO, Technical SEO, and Content Quality. When working together, these pillars will improve a website’s organic visibility and traffic.

**Are backlinks in SEO still important?**

Yes, backlinks in SEO still remain a significant ranking factor. However, their value is not the same as it was a few years ago. Google now looks for links that are authoritative, relevant, and trustworthy.

**What should businesses focus on to improve Google page rank?**

The most important ranking factors that a business should focus on are user experience, search intent alignment, E-E-A-T, engagement signals, and topical authority.

**Why do search rankings change so frequently?**

Organic rankings fluctuate because Google frequently launches algorithm updates. Algorithm updates introduce new guidelines or update the previous ones. This causes significant organic ranking shifts. However, by adhering to the guidelines and implementing the changes, you can retain the position in search results.

**What is the golden rule of SEO?**

The golden rule of SEO means creating content for users rather than search engines. This means providing clear, relevant, and engaging information to the audience.

web.description: These are the top SEO ranking factors to focus on if you want to improve search rankings, drive organic traffic, and succeed online.
web.markdown:
Do you want to learn proven strategies to keep your business visible in search results? When it comes to [search engine optimization](https://www.westcounty.com/seo/seo-overview) (SEO), staying ahead can feel like a relentless challenge. Search engines like Google and Bing use complex algorithms to determine which web pages deserve top positions. However, these algorithms are constantly updating, and SEO strategies have to adapt. So, if you want your website to rank higher, understanding the most important **SEO ranking factors** to focus on is essential.

That’s why we’ve created this handy guide to break down the most important SEO ranking factors in 2026. Whether you’re a small business owner or manage a large franchise, optimizing your website for these key factors can make a huge difference in your search rankings.

**In this guide, we explain:**

- [What “SEO ranking factors” means](https://www.westcounty.com/seo/top-seo-ranking-factors#defined)
- [What the top SEO ranking factors in 2026 are, including:](https://www.westcounty.com/seo/top-seo-ranking-factors#top-factors)
- [Content Quality, Originality, and Value](https://www.westcounty.com/seo/top-seo-ranking-factors#content-quality)
- [User Search Intent Alignment](https://www.westcounty.com/seo/top-seo-ranking-factors#search-intent)
- [Demonstrating E-E-A-T](https://www.westcounty.com/seo/top-seo-ranking-factors#eeat)
- [Website User Experience (UX)](https://www.westcounty.com/seo/top-seo-ranking-factors#ux)
- [Technical SEO and Site Performance](https://www.westcounty.com/seo/top-seo-ranking-factors#technical-seo)
- [How to boost and maintain your SEO rankings](https://www.westcounty.com/seo/top-seo-ranking-factors#contact-us)

## What Are SEO Ranking Factors?

[![SEO Ranking Factors concept. Flat design vector illustration with light blue background and two illustrated people moving search engine results up to rank higher on giant computer screen.](https://www.westcounty.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/top_seo_ranking_factors-3-1024x557.webp)](https://www.westcounty.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/top_seo_ranking_factors-3.webp)

First things first, _what are SEO ranking factors?_ **SEO ranking factors** are the criteria search engines use to determine where a webpage appears in search results.

When someone searches for something online, search engines use algorithms to sort through _billions_ of web pages to find and rank the most helpful results. _But [how do search engines rank web pages](https://www.westcounty.com/seo/seo-overview#how)?_ Great question!

Search engines like Google analyze a series of factors to determine which pages are **the most relevant** to that particular search. The better a webpage aligns with these specific factors, the more likely it is to rank.

However, Google considers over **200 ranking factors** to determine the relevance, quality, and usefulness of a webpage.1 So with literally _hundreds_ of ranking signals at play, knowing where to start can feel impossible, especially if you’re not an SEO expert.

**That’s where we come in.** If you want to boost your rankings, drive more organic traffic to your website, and set your business up for SEO success, _keep reading._

## Top SEO Ranking Factors in 2026

[![SEO Ranking Factors concept. Flat design vector illustration with white background and a computer screen with various](https://www.westcounty.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/top_seo_ranking_factors-7-1024x614.webp)](https://www.westcounty.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/top_seo_ranking_factors-7.webp)

Today’s search engines are smarter than ever, and they’re only getting more advanced. Platforms like Google and Bing use [AI-powered search algorithms](https://www.westcounty.com/seo/seo-overview#ai-experiences) to analyze and rank web pages. This is shaping the future of online search in ways we’re only just beginning to see.

Keeping up with algorithm updates and new technologies can be overwhelming, but it’s also _essential_ for SEO success.

While there’s no foolproof formula for ranking #1, we do know that some ranking factors have a much greater impact than others. Below, we share our top SEO ranking factors for 2026, all of which we’ve personally tested and implemented to drive success for [West County Net clients](https://www.westcounty.com/portfolio).

### Content Quality, Originality, and Value

High-quality, original content continues to be one of the most powerful ranking factors in 2026. Search engines like Google prioritize well-researched, comprehensive, and unique content that provides genuine value to users:

#### **Content Quality**

If the content on your website is well-written, informative, and easy to read, people are more likely to stay on the page longer, engage with it, and explore more of the site. This can signal to search engines that it’s high-quality.

#### **Content Originality**

Google and other search engines reward original and unique content. If a webpage just copies what’s already out there, it doesn’t add value and likely won’t stand out. Original perspectives, first-hand insights, or even just a fresh way of explaining something are _way more_ likely to stand out to both users and search engines.

#### **Content Value**

Your content should answer people’s questions, entertain them, and be useful overall. If users find what they’re looking for on your site, they’re more likely to trust it, share it, and come back for more. Search engines can recognize these signals and boost the visibility of these high-value web pages.

By providing high-quality, unique, and genuinely helpful information, you increase your chances of ranking higher in search results while also attracting people who are actively searching for what you offer.

### User Search Intent Alignment

When someone searches for something online, they typically have a specific goal or outcome in mind—whether it’s to find information, buy something, or navigate to a specific website. This is called **search intent**.

For example, if someone searches “how to bake a cake,” their search intent is likely to find a recipe, not a page selling pre-made baked goods.

More than anything, search engines want to deliver results that best match a user’s search intent. Businesses that create content aligned with their ideal customers’ needs and intent are more likely to appear in relevant search results.

**Different types of user search intent include:**

- **Informational:** When a user is looking for an answer to a question or wants to learn more about something in general.
- **Navigational:** When a user is searching for a specific website or webpage.
- **Commercial:** When a user is researching options before buying a product or service.
- **Transactional:** When a user is ready to act and purchase something.

By addressing the specific questions, pain points, needs, and interests of your target audience, you can position your website for long-term SEO success.

### Demonstrating E-E-A-T (Experience, Expertise, Authority, Trust)

The **E-E-A-T** framework continues to play a critical role in website rankings in 2026. E-E-A-T stands for experience, expertise, authoritativeness, and trustworthiness. Each element contributes to SEO rankings:

- **Experience:** Demonstrating firsthand experience or knowledge can improve credibility and trust.
- **Expertise:** Depth, accuracy, and reputable source citing help demonstrate the necessary qualifications and/or knowledge in the subject matter.
- **Authoritativeness:** Consistently publishing high-quality content that is referenced and linked to by other reputable sites helps establish authority in an industry.
- **Trustworthiness:** Factors like accuracy, honesty, safety, and reliability of a webpage establish trust with both users and Google.

#### **Your Money or Your Life** (YMYL) Topics

As a whole, this concept functions as the criteria for how Google defines quality content.2 This is especially true for topics that can impact a person’s well-being, such as their health, finances, and safety.2 These are known as **Your Money or Your Life** **(YMYL)** topics.

YMYL topics have particularly high E-E-A-T rating standards because low-quality content has the potential to negatively impact the welfare or well-being of people and society.2

### User Experience (UX)

Imagine walking into a store, and it’s a mess. You can’t find anything, nothing is labeled or tagged correctly, and the aisles are actually a giant maze. You’d probably leave, right? Search engines think the same way about websites.

Good **user experience (UX)** on a website is important for SEO because it can indicate to search engines how much people like visiting your site. The more useful and enjoyable your site is, the more Google will want to show it to people.

Some key website UX factors that can affect rankings include:

#### **Mobile-Friendliness**

More than 60% of Google searches are performed on mobile devices.3 So responsive websites that work well on both desktop and mobile formats are essential for online visibility.

#### **Easy Navigation**

A well-organized website structure ensures visitors can find what they need quickly without clicking through a maze of pages. It also helps search engines crawl (explore and gather information) and index (organize into a giant database) content more efficiently, aiding in rankings.

#### Fast **Page Loading Speed**

If a page takes too long to load, people are likely not going to stick around and wait. How quickly your website loads and responds to user actions can greatly impact your rankings. Faster loading times and a smooth user experience are generally rewarded by search engines because they contribute to overall user satisfaction.

#### **High User Engagement**

This is the percentage of visitors who stay on your website and interact with your content. These interactions (engagements) can include things like clicking links, scrolling, or visiting multiple pages. A high engagement rate signals to search engines that your content is valuable and useful, which can contribute to higher search rankings.

Good UX means people stay longer, explore multiple pages, and have a positive experience on your website. Search engines are much more likely to rank websites that provide a positive user experience.

### Technical SEO and Site Performance

As the name suggests, [technical SEO](https://www.westcounty.com/seo/seo-overview#technical-seo) ensures your website is technically sound, and your overall site performance is “healthy.” This ongoing backend work helps ensure your website is easy for people to use and for search engines to understand.

Important technical SEO factors that contribute to a website’s overall performance and rankings are:

#### **Crawling Errors**

Issues like broken links (404 errors), pages that aren’t linked from any other pages on your website (orphaned pages), and similar problems can affect rankings. These errors can negatively affect the user experience and hinder search engines’ ability to effectively crawl, index, and rank your website.

#### **SSL Certification (HTTPS)**

A Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) certificate tells both users and search engines that any sensitive data (like log-in or credit card information) is secure and protected on a website. This SSL certification is indicated by the “HTTPS” prefix in a website’s URL.

#### **Sitemap**

An XML sitemap is a file that helps guide search engine algorithms through your website’s structure, making it easier for them to crawl, index, and rank critical webpages. If search engines can’t properly crawl your website, they can’t effectively rank it either. By providing a clear list of your pages, sitemaps can help ensure that all your important content is crawled and indexed.

#### **Schema Markup (Structured Data)**

Schema markup, or structured data, is a special type of code that helps search engines understand the content of a page better. This understanding can make it easier to rank for relevant search queries and boost your chances of appearing in competitive search features like rich snippets, knowledge panels, and AI overviews.

A well-optimized site with strong Technical SEO and performance provides a smooth experience for users and search engines, both of which are essential for good search rankings.

## Learn How You Can Boost Your Website Rankings This Year and Beyond with a Free SEO Audit

[![a blue background with a cut out of a hand holding a magnifying glass up to a laptop computer screen and a large blue arrow pointing upward indicating success and growth](https://www.westcounty.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/SEO_Ranking_Factors_CTA-Banner-1024x432.webp)](https://www.westcounty.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/SEO_Ranking_Factors_CTA-Banner.webp)

SEO is constantly evolving, and what works today may not be as effective tomorrow. Search engines continue to refine their algorithms, user behavior shifts, and new technologies emerge. This makes a dynamic SEO strategy essential for adapting and maintaining strong rankings long-term.

If you want an SEO strategy that stays ahead of the curve, we are here to help. Contact our SEO specialists today to learn how we can boost your website rankings in 2026 and beyond!

[Figure it Out](https://www.linkbuildinghq.com/blog/?category=figure-it-out)

Published on January 26th, 2026

![Search Ranking](https://www.linkbuildinghq.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/How-To-Improve-Google-Search-Ranking-In-2026_blog_image.jpg)

# How To Improve Google Search Ranking In 2026

In SEO circles, there’s this notorious joke that “if you’re looking to hide a dead body, dump it on page 2.” It’s a real shame, to be honest. But also, the fact is that the 1st search ranking position on page one captures about 40% of a keyword’s [total CTR](https://backlinko.com/google-ctr-stats). Page 2? It gets just [0.63% of the click](https://backlinko.com/google-ctr-stats) share.

As a result, the popular question in SEO evolved from “ _How to get more traffic_?” into “ _How to rank higher on Google_?” There’s a correlation, of course. But seeing that a big share of search traffic is concentrated on the first page, employing a ranking strategy is the only viable way to fuel the traffic pipeline.

## What are Search Rankings & Why Do They Matter?

Search ranking refers to the position of a URL on a search engine’s results page. For example, for the keyword “hyperlocal seo”, we currently hold the first organic search ranking position.

![Search Rankings ](https://www.linkbuildinghq.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Picture1-1.png)

But why is it important for websites to rank?

The answer is simple. _To redirect more traffic to web pages for target keywords_.

However, rankings don’t just mean getting to the top of Google search anymore. 2026 is a new ballgame altogether — thanks to the introduction of AI Mode and expansion of AI Overviews. The battleground has just gotten brutal, and the new trophy is visibility.

Higher visibility isn’t contingent on ranking entirely, it also requires getting cited in the AI Overviews, AI Mode, and other search features like PAAs and Rich Snippets. At least, that’s the strategy we’ve been operating on. And that’s what got us appearing in AIOs for keywords like “what is SERP tracking.”

![Search Rankings](https://www.linkbuildinghq.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Picture2-1.png)

Recalibrating the search ranking principles can get you top spots for high-intent queries, like this one.

![what is SERP tracking](https://www.linkbuildinghq.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Picture3-1.png)

So if your broader objective is to rank higher on Google in 2026, the arrow should be pointed at high-visibility placements. That doesn’t just include organic search, but AIOs, AI Mode, and SERP features as well.

## How Does Google Choose Sources To Rank in 2026?

Google doesn’t look at your content as a single entity anymore; instead, the algo has been wired to pull up information in chunks. [Search Engine Journal](https://searchengineland.com/guide/content-chunking-seo) reiterates this point, saying:

“… _Google’s algorithms quickly assess topical depth by scanning your macro structure, extract specific answers from micro chunks for SERP features, and pull precise data points from atomic elements for AI Overviews_.”

That’s why matching search intent is the priority, especially in 2026.

When you search for a query, it compiles not just the ten blue links, but a range of other search features that can all trigger clicks. So, it’s time to look at the bigger picture.

Here’s a breakdown of exactly what the system does when picking sources to rank.

- A searcher searches on Google.
- The intent of the search is determined.
- The most relevant pages are identified and pushed to the top.
- Some of these pages appear in organic listings, and others are compiled within search snippets, overviews, merchant listings, and other SERP features.

Ranking on Google means optimizing for the broader search experience, not just the top 10 results. The new search realm caters to queries at their core, highlighting answers before links and directing links to specific passages rather than the page as a whole. In essence, much has changed, and these changes are characterized by the following search ranking signals.

### How Google Ranks Content in 2026

- Google matches intent at the passage level, which means content gets broken down into chunks and each piece is analyzed separately to rank at the micro-intent level.

- Content structure is more important than ever before because LLM chatbots trust text that’s easy to navigate and comes from trustworthy sources.

- A brand with a higher number of brand mentions is considered more authoritative. The game isn’t just about backlinks anymore, but what people really think of your brand across community platforms, social media, and forums.

- The algorithm uses page signals such as engagement rate, dwell time, and end-to-end user journey to determine how effectively a page addresses a query.

- The [language and nature of a query](https://www.google.com/intl/en_us/search/howsearchworks/how-search-works/ranking-results/) determine what type of results are returned. For example, a query regarding restaurants in the Arabic language will pull up restaurants in the local area with Arabic content on their websites.

## Our 7-Step Strategy To Rank on Google Search & Boost Visibility

Ranking is not a sprint; it takes time to strategize a plan that actually gets you to page one. After tests and trials with hundreds of content pieces, we’ve unlocked the most effective strategy to improve Google search rankings for any website.

### Step 1: Identify Best-Performing Pages

In marketing, they suggest kicking things off where the returns are already rolling in. See which pages on your site are already doing well and create a list of the top 20 pages to re-optimize. This is your success zone, and working on it is going to ensure the existing traffic gets boosted to a significant margin.

The first step is to [check website ranking on Google](https://www.linkbuildinghq.com/blog/best-seo-rank-checking-software-for-daily-rankings/) Search Console and identify top performers (pages ranking between 11th and 20th positions).

![ Identify Best-Performing Pages](https://www.linkbuildinghq.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Picture4.png)

These pages need a little push to get the traffic flowing. Follow these steps to begin:

1. Go to GSC dashboard and select your property.
2. Navigate to the performance report and scroll down to find “Pages.”
3. Export a CSV of this and narrow down the page count by separating the pages ranking in positions 11-20.
4. Identify their target keywords and start optimizing.

### Step 2: Analyze Competitors’ Content to Beat them at their own game

Now that we have a list of pages to target, get ready to aim for the top positions held by competitors. The goal is to find what is ranking on page one, identify gaps, and fill them to capture top positions.

### How to Run a Comprehensive Competitor Analysis

Benchmark the first 3 websites on the Google search ranking page. Assess these pages for ranking factors specific to content, user experience, and link profile. Here’s a step-by-step process to cover all bases.

- Assess the quality and depth of content. Is it written on EEAT standards? Is it generic or does it stack on topical relevance?


Review the content format and structure. Check if there are non-text elements used, like video, images, and other interactive elements.

- Analyze content gaps that can help you outrank them, such as a lack of visual elements, content that reads flat, or one that isn’t structured properly.

- Perform a backlink gap analysis on Semrush or other third-party tools to review which referring domains could be contributing to their rankings.

- Check page speed and mobile accessibility to identify technical gaps.

This is essential to uncover weaknesses in your and your competitors’ strategy, giving you the foundational groundwork to begin optimizing for improved search rankings.

### Step 3: Get The Basics (of SEO) Right

Traditional SEO never gets old, no matter what type of pages you’re trying to rank. Besides matching search intent, off-page factors can be critical in determining whether your page gets picked up by the algorithm or not. So, if you’ve been asking yourself how to improve SEO on Google, start with on-page and off-page optimization.

#### Sprinkle Relevant Keywords In Content

Answering search queries in content is the fundamental rule for creating SEO content. Targeting terms people use to search for your niche information is mandatory to help crawlers interpret the context of your pages. Google expands on this, [saying that](https://www.google.com/intl/en_us/search/howsearchworks/how-search-works/ranking-results/'):

_“To give you the most useful information, Search algorithms look at many factors and signals, including the words of your query, relevance and usability of pages, expertise of sources, and your location and settings.”_

Sprinkle relevant terms throughout the content to signal semantic relevance. Make sure that your heading, meta title, and description also contain the primary keyword to send powerful relevance signals. Use a descriptive URL and add keywords in the slug as well. Using a holistic approach to keywords ensures your content gets picked up for targeted queries.

#### Link Internally to supplement internal navigation

Internal linking is done correctly when it helps users on a site discover content in a more efficient manner. Including clear and descriptive anchor text helps, but relevance should not be overlooked.

The simple rule is to find which pages can actually help users find out more about a topic you’re referring to and link to it! Simple and effective.

#### Plan Topic Clusters to establish strong topical authority

Topic clusters are a huge part of building topical authority. They let you expand on a topic your expertise lies in, and then link it internally to pass link juice across the site.

Creating a topic cluster is pretty simple. You pick the core topic for the pillar page and brainstorm relevant keywords for it. The subtopics will be used as cluster pages for the broader topic. For example, if our pillar page is about dog food, the cluster pages could be.

Organic Dog Food

Best Protein Sources for Dogs

Vitamins & Minerals Dogs Need

Senior Dog Food

Puppy Food

Wet vs Dry food

Hypoallergenic Dog Food

Raw vs Cooked Homemade Dog Food

![Plan Topic Clusters to establish strong topical authority ](https://www.linkbuildinghq.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Picture5.jpg)

#### Strengthen Off-Page Factors

On-page optimization will only boost search rankings when combined with off-page efforts. Without it, it would be like optimizing an engine but never adding fuel. Plus, it’s a mandatory part of the SEO basics.

For off-page optimization, there are a few key principles to follow:

- Pages must be crawlable and indexable.
- Any pages blocked by robots.txt should be assessed and removed, if required.
- Building a strong backlink profile to strengthen domain authority.
- Optimizing for local searches (more on that later.)
- Brand mentions across niche communities, forums, and social media.

Nail these down before adding on advanced strategies to crawl up the SERPs.

### Step 4: Fulfill Content Signals

Google algorithm doesn’t just operate on a set of predefined ranking signals; it also picks up real-world signals like bounce rate and scroll depth. And these can impact your chances to rank, because they’re used to determine how helpful your content is to searchers.

So next time the question pops up in your mind: “how to boost my Google ranking?” Remember, the answer lies in satisfying advanced content signals like time on page, bounce back rate, and pages per session. We have explained the non-traditional metrics in [our recent blog](https://docs.google.com/document/d/13g_aXGOE62EotocTODBSmCNDUTMm0hDhYIyl9qmzsug/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.o0a2l4br4hcj), check it out to see what you should be tracking besides keywords.

If your content fails to hit content signals, it’s probably considered low value and may be ignored by the algorithm. But you can reverse that using the following tactics.

#### Answer key questions first

In the era of zero-click searches, you can’t expect people to scroll through an entire page to find an answer. If you want to surpass search features like AIOs and Discussions, lay out your answer right in the first paragraph.

A great way to do this is to first mention the question and directly answer it in the following sentence. Just like we did in [this blog](https://www.linkbuildinghq.com/blog/how-to-rank-in-people-also-ask-google-section/).

![Answer key questions first ](https://www.linkbuildinghq.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Picture6.png)

That’s how you lure in readers to keep digging. Lead with the answer and a promise to expand on the topic.

#### Use Clean, Scannable Formatting

Your content shouldn’t look like pieces of a puzzle scattered around an empty canvas. It should all make sense, have a clear structure, and be easy to skim.

Since LLMs and AI engines parse information to understand content, breaking it up into chunks is a great strategy. But make sure that each chunk relates to the information above and below. This way, it’ll be easier for AI and search crawlers to find answers in your content and cite it as a source.

#### Break Up Your Content with Visuals and Examples

Visuals diversify content and make it easier to consume. Essentially, these are supporting elements that come together to deliver value to users. Plus, no one wants to read a page that’s all text; the eye seeks diversity.

So, when creating pages, make sure it incorporates visual elements like

- Infographics explaining concepts and ideas in an easy-to-digest visual.
- Screenshots to give examples or elaborate on a subject in the text.
- Before-and-after illustrations of concepts and ideas.
- Charts and graphs to prove a point.

#### Leverage Videos for higher dwell time

Dwell time is part of the content satisfaction signal, and a video can significantly boost this metric. When you insert useful videos in your content, users spend more time on the page, signaling to search engines that this page is useful and valuable.

So instead of text-heavy pages, consider adding videos showcasing:

- Step-by-step guides to a process.
- Customer testimonials.
- Interview with experts or Q&A sessions.
- Brand stories, etc.

#### Update Content Regularly

If you analyze the SERPs extensively, you’ll realize that most organic listings are recently published pages. This is because the algorithm prioritizes fresh, updated content.

We’re not saying that; [Google is](https://www.google.com/intl/en_us/search/howsearchworks/how-search-works/ranking-results/):

“ _…when searching for current news topics, content freshness plays a bigger role than dictionary definitions_.”

This isn’t just limited to new websites. Niche sites should never rely on outdated content either. To keep your content current, regularly monitor pages that are losing search rankings, traffic, or clicks. Update these pages with the latest information, align them with current content standards, and incorporate the elements that are helping competitors gain traction.

## Step 5: Structure Content for Clarity & Skimmability

Next up, content structure.

You may have all the best elements for quality content—depth, visuals, user intent, and freshness—but unless it’s structured properly, search crawlers may not be able to scan it with accuracy.

Your page might still rank, but you’ll be missing out on high-visibility spots like AIO citations and the PAA snippets.

_“Write content naturally and make sure the content is well written, easy to follow, and free of spelling and grammatical mistakes.”_

- [SEO Starter Guide by Google](https://developers.google.com/search/docs/fundamentals/seo-starter-guide)

Use our tested content techniques to gain an edge on the ranking page.

#### Insert Table of Contents

Table of contents with jump links is perfect for long-form blogs. It helps scrollers and crawlers easily navigate through different sections of a blog or article. Moreover, it reinforces structural coherence across the page.

#### Add Headings in a Logical Order

For search engines, headings are checkpoints, helping users and crawlers understand context, hierarchy, and relevance at a glance. That’s why it’s important to follow a clear hierarchy to ensure users stay engaged. Plus, it also assists crawlers in interpreting each part of content and rank it in various search features.

A three-thumb rule we follow is: keep it **relevant, scannable**, and **neat**.

#### Use a Diverse Range of Content Formats

If you’re not sure how to increase Google ranking positions across different search features, we have a secret sauce to boost your visibility. Match search intent and target all search tabs, not just the “All” tab. Using a range of formats allows crawlers to gauge your content for different search tabs. So, consider adding content variations like:

- Tables
- Bullet points and numbered lists
- Images/Infographics/Diagrams
- Quotes and expert insights
- Callout boxes or key takeaways
- Videos
- FAQs

Content satisfaction signals build momentum over time and reinforce long-term search ranking performance. By adhering to the simple principles, you’ll significantly hike the satisfaction rate of both users and search engines.

### Step 6: Make Sure There Are No Technical Insufficiencies

Technical optimization is a critical part of the SEO puzzle, ensuring your UX is seamless and there are no glitches in the website operations. For businesses trying to improve Google search results, implementing technical SEO is a must.

#### Improve Site Speed

Site or page speed is a direct ranking factor. It’s listed in the Page Experience guidelines and considered an important part of the [Core Web Vitals](https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/core-web-vitals) metrics.

Page speed is basically a measure of a website’s loading performance. A slow loading speed drives up bounce back rate, signaling that your content does not fulfill the search intent.

Google provides an in-house tool to measure this speed and assess any insufficiencies. If the score is below 89 on [PageSpeed Insights](https://pagespeed.web.dev/?utm_source=psi&utm_medium=redirect), it may send negative satisfaction content signals and consequently hinder the ranking needles.

![Improve Site Speed ](https://www.linkbuildinghq.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Picture7.png)

#### Mobile Friendly

Besides other things, a mobile-friendly site reduces bounce rate. Since mobile devices account for [62.54 percent](https://www.statista.com/statistics/277125/share-of-website-traffic-coming-from-mobile-devices/?srsltid=AfmBOopoRVQexhQj30shZDQYoFVsqDMPi5Pwy2aCYkv-i5UP5jS3fSqf) of global website traffic, search engines associate mobile-friendliness with credibility and user satisfaction.

That is why a website that loads fast, has a responsive design, and is easy to use sends stronger ranking signals.

#### Other Technical Factors

There are other primary technical factors, which, if not optimized, can impact a website’s discoverability and ranking potential. Follow this checklist to review if your site is ready to rank, technical SEO-wise.

- Ensure pages are crawlable and indexable.
- Get a secure HTTPS connection (make your site SSL verified).
- Implement Schema to enable visibility in rich snippets.
- Remove intrusive dialog boxes or pop-ups.
- Use [supported image](https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/google-images#supported-image-formats) and video file formats.

### Step 7: Satisfy Local Search Metrics to Get visible for local searches

When regional businesses optimize for local keywords, it helps them appear on Google’s search features like:

- Local Pack
- Google Maps
- Google Business Profile (GBP)
- Knowledge Graph

For such keywords, Google returns results based on search intent and location. That means local search optimization allows you to improve visibility on local searches and appear for the above-mentioned search features.

But local search rankings are contingent on optimization strategies like:

#### Providing Complete Business Information

A partially optimized business profile leaves a lot of blind spots for search crawlers, which is likely sending negative signals from your profile. That is why a complete and up-to-date business profile is the first prerequisite to get listed in local search rankings.

Make sure your full business name, address, and phone number (NAP) are listed in the GBP. This should be consistent across all local directories. Mention any other specifications for your business, like working hours, Wi-Fi availability, and service details.

Google algorithm now picks up keywords from customer feedback or the comments section as well, so make sure these sections portray a positive brand image. Not just that, it seems Google is also picking up social media signals to highlight tags on local listings. [Darren Shaw](https://www.threads.com/@darrenshawseo/post/DTsx0-4j7fK) talked about this recently, and it’s quite an revolutionary move for local search.

#### Align On-Page Factors for Local Visibility

If you’re trying to rank for local search terms, it goes without saying that your content should be focused on the same. For example, a car maintenance shop in downtown Philadelphia will target keywords like “car maintenance philadelphia,” or “best mechanic near me” besides generic search terms like “car maintenance shop.”

For cross-country businesses, creating landing pages specific to each location is also a good practice and helps visibility on various local listings. Suppose you’re a tire supplier with many branches across the United States. Creating dedicated landing pages for each region is going to help redirect the target audience to your website.

#### Implement Local Schema:

One more way to rank higher on Google local search features is to implement local schema. There are different types of schemas local businesses can benefit from, including (a) Local Business Schema, (b) Review Schema, and (c) FAQ Schema.

This will ensure your business is eligible for rich results like this one.

![Implement Local Schema:](https://www.linkbuildinghq.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Picture8.png)

## The Closing Remarks

And there we go, a guide covering every optimization tactic under the sun to help sites rank. But the answer for how torank higher on Google search is one tied to the cause of low rankings, not just the listed search ranking factors above.

For some, updating content might do the trick, but others may need to implement a comprehensive technical audit to get the needle moving again. Simply put, the answer lies in the diagnostics. Figure out what you’re lacking and fix it to outrank competitors.
